handoff_goal: Specify Launchdeck Agent Integration as one shared local lifecycle control plane with a reusable Application Kernel, backward-compatible CLI adapter, typed local stdio MCP adapter, one canonical Agent Skill, and independently generated Codex and Claude Plugin packages.

agent_requirement_contract.target_need: Coding agents and developers need reliable local project lifecycle operations through CLI, Skill, MCP, and Plugins without duplicate services, unsafe process control, divergent state, or host-specific safety drift.
constraints: F:/github/launchdeck is the implementation target and source of truth for current behavior.
The existing daemonless user-scoped Launchdeck control plane remains authoritative.
CLI, MCP, and Plugin runtimes share LAUNCHDECK_HOME, registry, run index, locks, logs, events, and operation journal.
Application Kernel owns execution, policy, ownership, compatibility, recovery, and normalized result semantics.
CLI schemaVersion 1 remains backward compatible while Agent protocol is versioned separately.
First-release MCP is local stdio, works on already configured projects, and executes only low-risk public mutations.
Public Agent tools do not accept arbitrary command, env, cwd, force, risky cleanup, reset, or model-authored confirmation fields.
Node.js 20+ remains a first-release prerequisite; Plugins must not require global CLI, npx, or install scripts.
Codex and Claude Plugins are separate generated artifacts from one compatibility manifest.
No release-readiness claim may exceed its tested surface, host, or operating-system evidence.
success_criteria: CLI and MCP map the same operation to semantically equivalent outcome, resource state, effects, safety, error, next-action, and provenance fields.
A Plugin-started service survives host/MCP exit and remains observable and safely controllable through standalone CLI.
Repeated start reuses the same run and does not create a second process.
Unknown or external process ownership is inspect-only and never authorizes mutation.
Risk is enforced by Kernel across CLI and MCP; public MCP refuses medium, high, and destructive execution in the first release.
Mutation transport failure recovers through journal and live evidence or returns indeterminate without automatic retry or cross-surface replay.
Safe clean executes only a newly revalidated digest-bound plan.
Plugin update, disable, and uninstall do not stop managed services or delete shared state.
Skill, runtime, schemas, compatibility manifest, and host manifest share a verifiable build identity.
Real stdio protocol tests and separate real Codex/Claude host smokes validate installation, routing, project scope, update, uninstall, and shared-state behavior.
design_direction: One control plane with a shared Application Kernel and thin CLI/MCP adapters.
One canonical reference-driven launchdeck-agent Skill; MCP supplies typed operations while Skill supplies intent routing and workflow policy.
One npm runtime and two generated host Plugin artifacts from one compatibility manifest.
Local stdio MCP with a deliberately narrower public safety surface than the complete CLI.
optimal_solution_approach: Extract application orchestration from CLI-private handlers into an Operation Registry and normalized Kernel execution interface.
Separate operation outcome from resource lifecycle status and carry effects certainty, safety, provenance, and next actions.
Use operation IDs and a shared journal for recovery; provide resource-aware idempotency and explicit indeterminate outcomes rather than claiming absolute exactly-once.
Generate CLI help/mapping, MCP tools/schemas, and Plugin compatibility data from shared operation definitions where practical without making host manifests a second policy source.
Package a prebuilt single-file ESM stdio MCP runtime with the canonical Skill into independently validated Codex and Claude Plugins.

in: Application Kernel, Operation Registry, project resolver, risk engine, ownership checks, locks, operation journal, normalized results, effects certainty, provenance, and compatibility gates
backward-compatible CLI adapter and capabilities operation
typed local stdio MCP tools for capabilities, diagnosis, bounded observation, low-risk lifecycle mutation, reconcile, and digest-bound safe clean
read-only adoption inspection without configuration mutation or implicit start
canonical launchdeck-agent Skill with MCP-first routing and safe CLI fallback
compatibility manifest and separately generated Codex/Claude Plugin packages
operation-contract, adapter-equivalence, real stdio, fault-injection, Skill tool-sequence, host, update, uninstall, and platform verification
out: force-stop, project removal, risky clean, reset, arbitrary shell/env/cwd/force inputs, or permanent follow tools
remote Streamable HTTP MCP, OAuth, hooks, MCP UI, GUI/TUI, production or remote service management
native binaries and enterprise managed-policy implementation
deferred: adoption_apply after a separately reviewed plan-digest and confirmation contract
medium-risk execution after target hosts prove reliable human elicitation
remote MCP, enterprise policy distribution, hooks, UI, and native executable packaging

sp-specify.reason: The coherent cross-cutting product and safety scope requires formal specification after the user confirms the protected digest.

sp-quick.reason: The change spans Kernel architecture, persistent state, concurrency, lifecycle safety, MCP protocol, two Plugin hosts, packaging, compatibility, fault recovery, and cross-platform verification.

current_project_roles.scope: Launchdeck CLI, Application Kernel, MCP, canonical Skill, Plugin sources, compatibility schemas, docs, tests, and release evidence.
current_project_roles.notes: Current repository is the product implementation target.

current_project_roles.scope: Existing CLI, control plane, Agent Skill, installer, output contract, runtime state, ownership, locks, logs, events, and tests.
current_project_roles.evidence_source: live repository files and targeted passing tests
current_project_roles.notes: Project cognition was unavailable; live repository evidence is authoritative.

target_project_roles.scope: Same F:/github/launchdeck repository; no cross-project transfer.
target_project_roles.evidence_source: explicit user handoff after locked discussion boundary
target_project_roles.notes: External host specifications constrain adapters but are not implementation targets.

external_systems: local operating-system process, port, and filesystem surfaces

required_target_paths_to_verify: final MCP adapter and bundle entrypoints
final operation and compatibility schema locations

source_evidence.claim: Locks first-release goals, scope, safety requirements, MCP boundary, and success criteria.
Selects shared Kernel plus thin adapters and rejects CLI-spawn as the permanent architecture.
Records the locked target boundary, live-repository truth pass, evidence confidence, and official external authorities.
Documents the current daemonless global control plane, lifecycle commands, ownership safety, JSON contract, and Agent Skill installer.
Provides the current canonical intent router and lifecycle safety workflow.
Provides the existing cross-host Skill target matrix and conservative install behavior.
Shows current CLI parsing and private application orchestration that must not be duplicated in MCP.
Provides current success/failure/partial envelopes, compact output, public error semantics, and safe next actions.
Provide reusable registry, run, lock, ownership, inspect, event, log, and process primitives.
Thirty-three targeted installer, CLI envelope, compact output, and control-plane contract tests passed during the truth pass.

source_evidence.source: Codex official Skills, Plugins, and MCP documentation; Claude Code official Skills, Plugins, and MCP documentation; MCP specification
source_evidence.claim: Establishes current host packaging differences, local stdio support, Skill structure, structured tool outputs, annotations, and capability negotiation boundaries.

soft_unknowns.claim: Codex form elicitation was not established as a portable first-release baseline from reviewed official materials.
soft_unknowns.stop_and_reopen_condition: First release is required to execute medium-risk tasks.

soft_unknowns.claim: Exact bundled MCP path substitution and manifest fields require separate real-host verification for Codex and Claude.
soft_unknowns.latest_resolve_phase: before a host-specific Plugin readiness claim
soft_unknowns.stop_and_reopen_condition: A host cannot launch the bundled runtime without install scripts or a global CLI.

soft_unknowns.claim: Exact Agent Operation Result field names and individual MCP output schemas remain to specify.
soft_unknowns.stop_and_reopen_condition: CLI compatibility or MCP schema limitations invalidate outcome/resource separation.

soft_unknowns.claim: Operation-journal storage and retention defaults remain to specify.
soft_unknowns.stop_and_reopen_condition: Retention hides recovery evidence or grows without bound.

soft_unknowns.claim: User-level Codex Skill compatibility/migration between legacy ~/.codex/skills and current $HOME/.agents/skills needs an explicit non-destructive contract.
soft_unknowns.stop_and_reopen_condition: Migration could overwrite divergent user content or create ambiguous duplicate Skills.

settled_decisions: One shared Application Kernel is the execution, safety, compatibility, and recovery authority.
CLI and MCP are thin adapters; CLI remains independently usable and backward compatible.
One canonical launchdeck-agent Skill owns intent routing and MCP-to-CLI fallback policy.
First-release MCP is local stdio, works on configured projects, and publicly executes only low-risk mutation.
One npm runtime and two separately generated Codex/Claude Plugin artifacts share one compatibility manifest.
Plugin installation does not require global CLI but retains Node.js 20+ as a prerequisite.
Mutation recovery uses operation IDs, journal, locks, and live evidence; indeterminate work is never automatically retried.
preserved_assumptions: Existing Launchdeck registry, run index, ownership, locks, events, logs, and daemonless lifecycle behavior remain valid foundations.
The canonical Skill remains one router with progressive-disclosure references rather than many competing public Skills.
Host-specific manifest generation is safer than forcing one physical Plugin layout.
conflicts_requiring_return: Making separate CLI and MCP control-plane state authoritative.
Allowing MCP or Skill to bypass Kernel ownership, risk, compatibility, or journal gates.
Making arbitrary shell execution or external-process termination part of public MCP.
Requiring automatic adoption writes or medium-plus MCP execution in the first release.
Making a daemon, remote MCP, hook, UI, native binary, or enterprise policy implementation mandatory for the first release.
Treating Plugin uninstall as service stop or shared-state cleanup.
capability_map: Kernel operation registry and normalized result contract
canonical Skill routing and safe fallback
compatibility manifest and dual host packaging
protocol, fault, Skill-eval, host, update, uninstall, and platform verification
dependencies: existing global registry, run index, locks, ownership proof, events, logs, runtime state, and process/port adapters
Node.js 20+ and a bundled MCP SDK/runtime
MCP stdio framing and structured tool result support
Codex and Claude Plugin packaging and host path resolution
stable compatibility manifest and component digests
planning_constraints: Do not prescribe an ordered implementation sequence in the specification contract.
Preserve existing CLI envelope fields while versioning Agent protocol separately.
Generate or validate adapter schemas from shared operation definitions to prevent drift.
Treat MCP annotations and host approvals as defense-in-depth, never as the Kernel safety boundary.
Bind all release claims to exact surface, host, operating-system, update, uninstall, and fault-recovery evidence.
deferred_scope: force-stop, risky clean, reset, and arbitrary shell inputs
remote MCP, OAuth, hooks, MCP UI, GUI/TUI
native binaries and enterprise managed-policy implementation
reopen_conditions: Existing control-plane modules cannot support a shared Kernel without changing confirmed safety semantics.
A target host cannot launch the bundled stdio runtime under the selected Node.js prerequisite and manifest model.
CLI backward compatibility prevents separating operation outcome from resource lifecycle status.
Reliable recovery requires a daemon or alternate state authority.
User requires a deferred high-risk, adoption-write, remote, UI, native, or enterprise capability in the first release.

locked_direction: One Launchdeck control plane exposed through Kernel, CLI, canonical Skill, local stdio MCP, and generated Codex/Claude Plugins.
Kernel owns execution, safety, state compatibility, recovery, and normalized semantics.
First release supports configured local projects and low-risk public MCP mutation only.
Plugins are installable without global CLI, share LAUNCHDECK_HOME, and do not own managed-service lifetime.
rejected_alternatives: broad MCP parity with arbitrary CLI execution
automatic adoption writes and medium-plus first-release MCP execution
accepted_tradeoffs: Node.js 20+ remains required instead of adding native binary distribution now.
Public MCP is narrower than CLI to preserve Agent safety.
Codex and Claude are separately packaged and verified to preserve host correctness.
Resource-aware recovery and indeterminate outcomes replace an impossible universal exactly-once promise.
Adoption inspection is read-only and configuration application is deferred.
experience_commitments: Users express lifecycle intent without naming Launchdeck tools.
Agents observe before mutate and report existing runs instead of duplicating them.
Failures explain project, task, actual runtime/surface, evidence, refusal reason, and safe next action.
Plugin update or uninstall never unexpectedly stops a local service or deletes its evidence.
review_criteria_carried_forward: single authoritative state and safety Kernel
no duplicate start and no unknown/external mutation
no automatic retry after indeterminate mutation
backward-compatible CLI plus typed MCP equivalence
real stdio and separate real-host verification
update/uninstall preserve services and shared state
must_not_dilute: Plugin/MCP process lifetime separate from managed task lifetime
Skill business refusals and uncertain mutations never trigger blind CLI fallback

must_preserve.claim: Launchdeck remains one authoritative local lifecycle control plane across CLI, Skill, MCP, and Plugins.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: The specification must not create a second backend, state namespace, or safety authority.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: Any surface becomes an independent lifecycle authority.

must_preserve.claim: F:/github/launchdeck is the implementation target and current behavior authority.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: All implementation and verification paths remain bound to the current repository.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: Implementation moves to another repository or package boundary.

must_preserve.claim: First release is configured-project, low-risk, local stdio MCP with one canonical Skill and dual generated host Plugins.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Scope and acceptance criteria preserve this release boundary.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: A deferred risk, adoption, remote, UI, native, or enterprise surface becomes mandatory.

must_preserve.claim: CLI compatibility and Agent protocol are separate versioned contracts.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Specification preserves CLI schemaVersion 1 while defining a normalized Agent Operation Result.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: Agent semantics require breaking existing CLI consumers.

must_preserve.claim: One canonical launchdeck-agent Skill remains intent-driven, reference-based, MCP-first, and safety-preserving.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Do not split first release into competing public lifecycle Skills or allow blind fallback.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: Users must select low-level tools or duplicate Skill copies drift.

must_preserve.claim: Plugin installation, update, disable, and uninstall are independent from managed-service and shared-state lifetime.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Acceptance criteria prove services and state survive host and Plugin lifecycle changes.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: Plugin lifecycle can implicitly stop services or remove state.

must_preserve.claim: Mutation recovery is journaled and evidence-based; indeterminate work is not automatically retried.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Specification includes operation IDs, journal state, retry semantics, and fault-injection acceptance.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: A transport failure can replay mutation through MCP or CLI.

must_preserve.claim: Release readiness is scoped by surface, host, OS, update, uninstall, and recovery evidence.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: The spec defines separate readiness claims and prevents CLI evidence from proving Plugin readiness.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: A broad ready claim lacks exact supporting evidence.

must_preserve.claim: CLI and MCP share the same safety Kernel; MCP cannot bypass ownership proof.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Map every mutation adapter to the same Kernel policy and ownership checks.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: Any MCP handler mutates state outside Kernel.

must_preserve.claim: MCP annotations are hints; deterministic Kernel controls provide real authorization.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Annotations never replace policy, ownership, risk, or confirmation checks.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: Tool annotations authorize mutation.

must_preserve.claim: Starting an already matching task returns its existing run instead of duplicating it.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Specify duplicate detection and reusedExistingRun semantics.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: Repeated start can spawn a second service.

must_preserve.claim: External and unknown process owners remain inspect-only.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: All mutation tools refuse without Launchdeck ownership proof.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: Port or PID alone authorizes mutation.

must_preserve.claim: Risky cleanup requires a plan/digest/confirmation boundary and is not exposed as a model-authored yes flag.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: First release exposes only digest-bound safe clean and defers risky clean.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: MCP can trigger risky deletion with a boolean input.

must_preserve.claim: MCP logs/events are bounded reads or polling, never permanent follow calls.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Schemas include bounds/cursors and exclude arbitrary paths or unbounded streams.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: A tool call can remain open indefinitely.

must_preserve.claim: Plugin runtime and standalone CLI verify state/schema compatibility and fail closed for unsafe writes.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Capabilities report actual runtime and compatibility before mutation.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: An older runtime writes newer shared state.

must_preserve.claim: Task risk is dynamically enforced by Kernel, not inferred only from static MCP annotation.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Every operation resolves current configured risk before execution.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: Configured risk remains display-only.

must_preserve.claim: Every MCP operation explicitly resolves project context and never defaults to Plugin installation directory.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Define projectRef/projectRoot/host-root precedence and missing/ambiguous errors.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: An operation can target the wrong directory implicitly.

must_preserve.claim: Plugin runtime, standalone CLI, registry, and run state use explicit compatibility checks and incompatible writes fail closed.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Separate package, Agent protocol, output, config, registry, and journal version axes.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: Package version alone decides write safety.

must_preserve.claim: An uncertain MCP mutation result cannot automatically fall back to CLI retry.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Fallback state machine inspects/reconciles uncertain work before any new operation.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: Transport failure can replay mutation.

must_preserve.claim: All operation results carry execution provenance.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Results identify surface, host, runtime kind/version/path, state home, and protocol version as applicable.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: Users cannot identify which runtime executed an action.

must_preserve.claim: Safe clean is bound to a revalidated plan digest.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Kernel recomputes the plan and rejects preview/execute drift.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: Changed targets can be deleted under an old preview.

must_preserve.claim: Agent task tools never accept arbitrary command, cwd, env, or force parameters.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Tool schemas select only declared projects/tasks and bounded options.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: MCP becomes a general shell wrapper.

must_preserve.claim: Plugin update and uninstall never migrate or delete shared control-plane state implicitly.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: State changes require explicit Launchdeck lifecycle/state operations.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: Plugin lifecycle mutates shared state.

must_preserve.claim: Task risk becomes a Kernel execution gate shared by CLI, MCP, and Plugins.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Current display-only risk gap is closed at the application boundary.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: Any surface bypasses task risk policy.

must_preserve.claim: Host approval and MCP annotations do not replace Kernel risk decisions.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Policy remains deterministic even when hosts auto-approve tools.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: Host configuration elevates Kernel permissions.

must_preserve.claim: Medium risk requires declared client elicitation and an accepted response; first release defers this capability.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Public MCP v1 rejects medium risk while preserving a future capability-negotiated contract.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: Medium-risk execution becomes first-release scope.

must_preserve.claim: Future confirmation binds to an operation digest and execution revalidates target, lock, port, and ownership.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Preserve prepare/confirm/revalidate semantics even though medium risk is deferred.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: A confirmed operation can execute changed inputs or state.

must_preserve.claim: Plugin installation scope and executable project scope are distinct; project Plugins cannot cross project roots.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Define project-scope violations and registered-project rules for user scope.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: Project Plugin can target unrelated projects.

must_preserve.claim: MCP server lifetime does not control managed-task lifetime.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Host/MCP exit leaves Launchdeck-owned services running and observable.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: Closing the host implicitly stops a managed service.

must_preserve.claim: Plugin disable, update, or uninstall preserves shared control-plane state.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Host lifecycle tests verify registry, journal, logs, events, config, and services remain.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: Plugin removal destroys recovery evidence.

must_preserve.claim: Standalone and Plugin Skills detect duplicate installation and content drift.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Installer doctor compares canonical content digests without overwriting divergent user files.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: Two Skills issue conflicting routing or safety guidance.

must_preserve.claim: Normalized results separate operation outcome from resource lifecycle status.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Define unambiguous outcome.kind and resource.status fields.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: One status field ambiguously means protocol and lifecycle state.

must_preserve.claim: CLI envelope and Agent protocol are independently versioned.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Compatibility checks do not conflate package, CLI, Agent, config, registry, or journal versions.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: A protocol change silently breaks CLI automation.

must_preserve.claim: A plan digest is integrity evidence, not a user-confirmation proof.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Specification separates plan validation from human interaction evidence.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: Possessing a digest authorizes mutation.

must_preserve.claim: MCP inputs cannot contain model-forgeable confirmation fields.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Exclude confirmed/yes/approval token fields from public tool schemas.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: The model can self-assert human approval.

must_preserve.claim: Every mutation is operation-journaled for retry and recovery safety.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Define journal states, operation IDs, input digest, result references, and retention.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: A Server restart loses mutation certainty.

must_preserve.claim: Indeterminate foreground task execution is never automatically rerun.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: task.run recovery distinguishes unknown completion from safe retry.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: A possibly side-effecting task runs twice.

must_preserve.claim: Future adoption writes can only apply a Kernel-generated and revalidated adoption plan.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: First release keeps adoption inspect-only and preserves safe deferred semantics.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: Automatic adoption writes enter first-release scope.

must_preserve.claim: Adoption and task start are distinct mutations and cannot be implicitly chained.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Read-only discovery never starts a server; future apply returns before start.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: Configuration acceptance automatically launches a task.

must_preserve.claim: npm runtime and host Plugins are different artifacts generated from one compatibility manifest.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Define one build identity and artifact-specific packaging contracts.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: Artifact metadata or behavior drifts between builds.

must_preserve.claim: Plugins carry a prebuilt MCP runtime and do not depend on global CLI, npx, or install-time scripts.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Plugin startup command points to bundled runtime with bundled dependencies.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: Plugin installation requires arbitrary code execution or PATH resolution.

must_preserve.claim: First-release installability explicitly requires Node.js 20+.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Plugin prerequisites and diagnostics report Node compatibility clearly.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: Product claims zero-runtime-dependency installability without native binaries.

must_preserve.claim: Skill, runtime, schemas, compatibility manifest, and Plugin manifest share one build identity and digests.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Capabilities fail writes on component digest mismatch.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: New instructions drive an old runtime or schema.

must_preserve.claim: MCP stdio stdout contains only valid protocol messages.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: All banners, diagnostics, and task output go to stderr or bounded state/log channels.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: Any non-protocol text contaminates stdout framing.

must_preserve.claim: Plugin update, disable, and uninstall do not affect managed services or shared state.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Update/uninstall acceptance tests prove lifetime separation.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: Host package lifecycle changes product runtime state.

must_preserve.claim: Host manifests do not duplicate risk, safety, state, or operation semantics.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Manifests contain identity, component paths, metadata, prerequisites, and presentation only.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: A host manifest becomes a conflicting policy source.

must_preserve.claim: CLI cross-platform smoke does not prove MCP or Plugin cross-platform compatibility.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Define separate surface/host/platform evidence matrices.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: A Plugin claim relies only on CLI smoke.

must_preserve.claim: Codex and Claude Plugins are independently verified before cross-host readiness.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Each host has installation, routing, MCP, scope, update, and uninstall evidence.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: One host's smoke is generalized to another.

must_preserve.claim: Adapter equivalence compares normalized semantics rather than raw host formatting.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Shared contract cases compare operation, outcome, resource, effects, safety, errors, next actions, and provenance.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: Brittle snapshots replace semantic contract proof.

must_preserve.claim: Launchdeck does not claim universal exactly-once mutation semantics.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Product language distinguishes recoverable idempotency from indeterminate side effects.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: Specification promises impossible exactly-once behavior.

must_preserve.claim: Retrying the same operation ID returns or recovers the original operation without re-execution.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Journal state and recovery logic are keyed by operation ID and input digest.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: Same-ID retry can repeat side effects.

must_preserve.claim: Indeterminate mutation cannot be automatically retried or replayed across surfaces.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Skill, CLI, and MCP all stop and inspect/reconcile when certainty is unavailable.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: Uncertain work is silently attempted again.

must_preserve.claim: Skill evaluation verifies actual tool-call sequences and forbidden calls, not only final prose.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Eval cases assert MCP-first selection, safe fallback, refusal handling, and no raw process control.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: A plausible answer hides unsafe calls.

must_preserve.claim: MCP release verification uses real stdio framing.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Automated protocol clients cover initialize, tools/list, tools/call, schema, output, stderr/stdout, interruption, restart, and concurrency.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: Only direct handler tests support an MCP-ready claim.

must_preserve.claim: A new Plugin version observes and safely manages services started by an older compatible version.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Update acceptance binds run ID, PID, port, state home, and compatible stop behavior across versions.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: Update duplicates or loses an existing run.

must_preserve.claim: Plugin uninstall preserves services and shared state and standalone CLI can take over.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Uninstall acceptance covers service liveness, state/log/event presence, CLI inspect/log/stop, and reinstall recovery.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: Uninstall strands or deletes managed state.

must_preserve.claim: Codex and Claude each require real host acceptance rather than manifest-only mocks.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Host-specific smoke is a named release gate.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: A host-ready claim lacks a real installation/use trace.

must_preserve.claim: Release claims bind to exact surface, host, OS, and verification evidence.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Define layered readiness labels and prohibit unsupported roll-up claims.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen_condition: Marketing or docs claim broader readiness than verified.
